audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar silver-trumpet and Sinai-departure text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for assembly signals, journey alarms, war remembrance, festival trumpets, departure order, Hobab invitation, ark guidance, and Moses' rise/rest prayers. Anti-militarization and anti-coercion safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Make for yourself two silver trumpets; hammered work you shall make them. They shall be for summoning congregation and for setting out camps.
When they blow both, whole congregation shall assemble to you at entrance of tent of meeting.
If they blow one, leaders, heads of thousands of Israel, shall assemble to you.
When you sound alarm/blast, camps camping eastward shall set out.
When you sound second alarm/blast, camps camping southward shall set out; alarm/blast shall be sounded for their journeys.
But when assembly is gathered, you shall blow, but not sound alarm/blast.
Sons of Aaron, priests, shall blow trumpets; they shall be for you as age-long statute throughout generations.
When you go to war in your land against adversary oppressing you, you shall sound alarm with trumpets, and you shall be remembered before YHWH your Divine and delivered from your enemies.

On days of your gladness, at appointed times, and at beginnings of months, you shall blow trumpets over burnt offerings and sacrifices of peace/well-being; they shall be for you as remembrance before your Divine. I am YHWH your Divine.
In second year, in second month, on twentieth of month, cloud lifted from over dwelling-place of testimony.
Children of Israel set out by their journeys from wilderness of Sinai, and cloud settled in wilderness of Paran.
They set out first at mouth/command of YHWH by hand of Moses.
Standard of camp of sons of Judah set out first by their hosts, and over his host was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
Over host of tribe of sons of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar.
Over host of tribe of sons of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
Dwelling-place was taken down, and sons of Gershon and sons of Merari set out carrying dwelling-place.
Standard of camp of Reuben set out by their hosts, and over his host was Elizur son of Shedeur.
Over host of tribe of sons of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
Over host of tribe of sons of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
Kohathites set out carrying holy things, and dwelling-place was set up before they arrived.
Standard of camp of sons of Ephraim set out by their hosts, and over his host was Elishama son of Ammihud.
Over host of tribe of sons of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
Over host of tribe of sons of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
Standard of camp of sons of Dan set out as rear guard of all camps by their hosts, and over his host was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
Over host of tribe of sons of Asher was Pagiel son of Ocran.
Over host of tribe of sons of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
These were settings-out of children of Israel by their hosts; so they set out.

Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, relative/in-law of Moses: We are setting out to place of which YHWH said, I will give it to you. Come with us, and we will do good to you, for YHWH has spoken good concerning Israel.
He said to him, I will not go, but to my land and my kindred I will go.
Moses said, Please do not leave us, for you know our camping in wilderness and you can be to us as eyes.
If you go with us, then the good with which YHWH does good to us, we will do good to you.
They set out from mountain of YHWH a journey of three days, and ark of covenant of YHWH journeyed before them a journey of three days to seek out rest for them.
Cloud of YHWH was over them by day when they set out from camp.
When ark set out, Moses said: Rise, YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered; let those hating you flee before you.
When it rested, he said: Return, YHWH, to myriads of thousands of Israel.

translation_notes: The trumpets distinguish assembly, travel, alarm, celebration, and worship; sound organizes communal attention.
War language belongs to ancient Israel's land-conflict setting and must not be generalized into religious militarism or aggression.
Tribal departure follows the camp order of Numbers 2 and the transport duties of Numbers 4.
Hobab is invited because of wilderness knowledge; his stated refusal must not be erased. Moses asks again and offers shared good rather than ownership.
The ark seeks rest for the people, and Moses' paired prayers hold movement and return together.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 10 to glorify war, force outsiders into the community, or confuse religious signals with unquestionable political commands. Its strongest pattern is coordinated movement, shared expertise, remembrance, celebration, and Presence seeking rest for the people.
